title: Dathomir Imperial Prison
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Structure |image=[[File:dathprison.jpg]] |name=Dathomir Imperial Prison |constructed=[[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'' |destroyed= |type= |location=[[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]] |interest=[[X1's Dathomir base]]''[[Star Wars Battlefront: Elite Squadron]]'' |rebuilt= |builder=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] |height= |width= |affiliation=*Galactic Empire *[[Nightsisters/Legends|Nightsisters]]''[[The Courtship of Princess Leia]]'' *[[X1's faction]] *[[Imperial Remnant]]{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20031120nightsaber|text=Nightsaber|int=Nightsaber}} }} {{Quote|That prison steals the mind! I will kill you before I go back!|An unidentified mercenary warlord|{{GalaxiesAED}}}} '''Dathomir Imperial Prison''' was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Prison/Legends|prison]] complex located on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] sometime during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. ==Description== {{Quote|Political prisoners?''"
"''And conscientious objectors. Potentially too valuable to the Empire to lose and too dangerous to let remain free and join the rebellion.''"
"''That's why the Empire imprisoned them here, on a virtually uncharted planet. If they were dangerous felons, they'd be sent to a maximum security facility where the Empire could boast that they'd never escape. But these are people that the Empire wanted simply to disappear.|[[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]|}} [[File:Dathomir_Imperial_Prison.jpg|thumb|left|220px|An AT-ST patrolling around the prison.]] The Dathomir Imperial Prison was a maximum-security prison for political prisoners considered too valuable to the Empire to be executed but who could not imprisoned in a facility known to the public.''[[Cracken's Threat Dossier]]'' These prisoners were thought to be some of the most dangerous to the Empire, and as such were left in this remote facility to be forgotten about.{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=poi|Imperial Prison|Dathomir}} The Dathomir prison was a typical monotonous and sharp-cornered Imperial construction made of [[Ferrocrete/Legends|ferrocrete]] and [[Plasteel/Legends|plasteel]]. The lower levels of he main building contained the prisoner cell blocks and a hangar while the upper levels housed administrative offices, kitchens, and barracks. Thousands of prisoners could be detained in the prison. The prison was surrounded by walls and protected by [[Laser turret/Legends|laser turrets]]. It was guarded by a regiment of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] and several [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-STs]] were also stationed here. ==History== {{Quote|I believe the Emperor himself was afraid of the Nightsisters. That's why he interdicted this planet. Years ago, he started a nice little penal colony here, not knowing about the Nightsisters. When he learned about them, he blew the planet's airfield from orbit and stranded hundreds of his own people here, along with the prisoners, rather than risk letting Gethzerion escape.|Leia Organa, to Han Solo|''[[The Courtship of Princess Leia]]''}} The planet Dathomir had a long history of use as a penal colony. The [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] had used the planet as a place to send various criminals. These people were left on the planet with no possible means of escape to live out the rest of their lives in solitude, struggling against the ferocious wildlife of the planet.[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] Around [[600 BBY/Legends|600 BBY]] when the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Allya/Legends|Allya]] was exiled to the planet by the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]]—her descendants became the [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] natives known as the [[Witches of Dathomir]].''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'' Around the time of the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the Empire established a presence on the planet Dathomir in order to restore the ancient penal colony. While [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] was aware of the presence of the native [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Witches of Dathomir]] on the planet, he allowed the construction of the prison. The complex was overseen by Warden [[Vinzel Haylon]] and the [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Imperial Inquisitor]] [[Olof]]. [[Mozo Bondog]] served as [[Chief of Security/Legends|chief of security]]. [[Xarot Korlin]] also worked in the prison. Then, it was not long before the [[Nightsisters/Legends|Nightsisters]], a sect of evil Dathomiri Witches, approached the Imperial [[Commander/Legends|commander]]. Their leader Gethzerion offered the services of her clan to control prisoners. Although the commander was quite suspicious about her true intentions, he finally accepted her offer. Gethzerion and her dark minions thus became a familiar sight in the Imperial compound. Using the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], the Nightsisters began to [[Mind trick/Legends#Force corrupt|corrupt]] the Imperial troops and progressively usurped control of the prison staff from Imperial officers. When the Empire discovered the situation, Gethzerion and the Nightsisters turned Imperial troops against their officers. A [[Invasion of Dathomir|battle]] therefore erupted on Dathomir between loyal Imperial forces and the Nightsisters backed by their Imperial [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]]. Eventually, the Nightsisters were defeated and repelled from the prison.{{InsiderCite|65|Who's Who in Star Wars Galaxies|Who's Who in ''Star Wars'' Galaxies}} [[File:rarepainting.jpg|thumb|right|200px|A [[Dathomir Prison|painting]] of the Dathomir Prison]] [[Dolac Legasi]] was incarcerated in the Imperial Prison at one time, as well as [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt's]] top [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] [[Imo Vledmo]], the [[Rebel pilot/Legends|Alliance pilot]] [[Gabriel Ian]]. At some point, the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]] [[Imarrra]] and [[Orrekazzapirr]] were captured by the Empire on [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] and brought to the prison on Dathomir.{{SWGcite|exp=RotW|type=faction|Rebel|To the queen's aid|Naboo}} In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],{{SWG_timeline}} a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] sent by Queen [[Kylantha]] of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] managed to infiltrate the facility and helped them to escape. The same year, [[CR90 corvette/Legends|Corellian corvette]] of the Rebel Alliance was captured by the Empire and the surviving crew was incarcerated on Dathomir. A Rebel agent sent by [[Master Sergeant/Legends|Master Sergeant]] [[Crowley (Twi'lek)|Crowley]] later infiltrated the prison to find information about the capture of the ship.{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=other|Corellian Corvette Pre-Quest: "Master Sergeant Crowley"}} In the meantime, Rebel officer [[Adar Tallon/Legends|Adar Tallon]] dispatched an operative to the facility in order to retrieve the security override codes of a corvette that was carrying a captured highly-decorated Alliance pilot.{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=other|Corellian Corvette Pre-Quest: "Adar Tallon"}} Due to the threat posed by the Nightsisters, [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] eventually decided maintaining the Imperial forces at Dathomir was not worth the risk of the Witches possibly escaping the planet. Shortly after the Battle of Yavin, the prison was subsequently [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|bombarded]] from [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] by [[Captain/Legends|captain]] [[Zsinj]], destroying every vessels parked there.''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' All communication equipment present on the planet was also destroyed. Dathomir was [[Blockade of Dathomir|blockaded]] by Imperial starships with strict orders to never land on the planet. The prison, including Imperial troops who survived the bombardment, were then completely abandoned by the Empire. The staff of the prison continued to fight the Nightsisters for years but they progressively fall under the control of Gethzerion and her dark followers. Then, the Imperial prison was ruled by the Nightsister [[War Chief]] [[Robetheri]] who set up a throne in the warden's office. In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],{{SWG_timeline}} several prisoners, including a [[Janta]] clan leader, a [[Teräs Käsi/Legends|Teräs Käsi]] master and a [[Unidentified mercenary warlord|Lokkian mercenary warlord]] succeeded in escaping the prison. This prompted Chief of Security Mozo Bondog to recruit Imperial agents to kill the fugitives.{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=static|Mozo Bondog|Dathomir}} The same year, [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] [[Lucia (smuggler)|Lucia]] and [[Tylar]] conducted business from within the confines of the prison walls.{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Smuggler|Smuggle Contraband|Dathomir}} In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], the [[Heroes of Yavin/Legends|Heroes of Yavin]] discovered the prison while they were trapped on Dathomir by [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] Zsinj. Allied with the [[Singing Mountain Clan]], they eventually defeated the Nightsisters and their Imperial slaves, liberating every prisoners. In the meantime, a [[Hapan Royal Navy|Hapan fleet]] broke the blockade of the planet and destroyed Zsinj's fleet. Both Zsinj and Gethzerion were killed in the [[Battle of Dathomir (Hunt for Zsinj)|battle]]. Sometimes after the defeat of Zsinj, the prison was reused by the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[X1]] who deployed a contingent of his [[X1's faction|Imperial troops]] and built an underground [[X1's Dathomir base|cloning center]] below the prison. [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[X2]] and [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] [[Grey Squadron]] later [[Mission to Dathomir (Galactic Civil War)|raided]] the prison in order to find information about the location of the [[X1's Fortress|main base]] of X1. The prison was then used to detain the Dathomiri Witches of the [[Dreaming River Clan]] during the [[Imperial Remnant|Imperial]] invasion of Dathomir in [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]]. ==Appearances==
